tasks.label: Formal rewriting
tasks.description: Transform casual English into formal English
tasks.testInput: Hey, just wanted to check in about the thing we talked about last week. Can we meet up soon?

examples.input: I wanna grab a coffee and chat.
examples.output: I would welcome the opportunity to meet for coffee and discuss the matter.

examples.input: Can you send me that report asap? It's kinda urgent.
examples.output: I would be grateful if you could forward the report at your earliest convenience, as the matter is time-sensitive.

examples.input: Sorry I missed your call — I was super busy.
examples.output: Please accept my apologies for missing your call; I was engaged with a prior commitment.

responses.0: Hey, just wanted to check in about the thing we talked about last week. Can we meet up soon? (No transformation — not enough examples to establish the pattern.)
responses.1: I would like to follow up regarding the matter we discussed last week. Would you be available to meet in the near future?
responses.2: I am writing to follow up on the matter we discussed during our previous conversation. I would appreciate the opportunity to arrange a meeting at a mutually convenient time.
responses.3: I am writing to follow up regarding the subject of our discussion last week. I would welcome the opportunity to meet at a time that is mutually convenient in order to progress this matter further.

tasks.label: 5-word summary
tasks.description: Summarise any text in exactly five words
tasks.testInput: The European Space Agency has announced plans to launch a new satellite to monitor global forest cover, deforestation rates, and carbon storage across all continents.

examples.input: Scientists have discovered a new species of deep-sea fish that produces its own light and has never been seen before.
The city council voted last night to ban single-use plastics from all council-owned premises starting next January.
A team of archaeologists has uncovered what they believe to be the remains of a Viking settlement in northern Scotland, dating back to the 9th century.

responses.0: ESA is planning to launch a satellite to monitor global forest cover and deforestation across continents.

tasks.label: Translate to Scots
tasks.description: Translate standard English into Scottish vernacular
tasks.testInput: I'm going to the shops to get some bread. Do you want anything?

examples.input: Hello, how are you doing today?
examples.output: Awricht, how ye deein the day?

examples.input: It's very cold outside. I think it might snow.
examples.output: It's awfie cauld ootside. Ah think it micht snaw.

examples.input: Don't worry about it — it's not a big deal.
examples.output: Dinnae fash yersel — it's nae a big deal.

responses.0: I'm going to the shops to get some bread. Do you want anything?
responses.1: Ah'm awa tae the shops tae get some breid. Dae ye want onything?
responses.2: Ah'm awa tae the shops tae get some breid. Dae ye want onythin?
responses.3: Ah'm awa tae the shoppies tae pick up some breid. Dae ye want onythin frae there?
